Setup and procedure of this experiment
Deitals of 7Be sample preparation and characterization
Neutron flux determination
nTOF facility (EAR2)
20 GeV/c proton beam on a Pb target
7Be (1.03+/-0.3 GBq, with 1% impurity due to 7Li)
radiocheimcally separated at PSI, and implanted on an
Al backing at CERN ISOLDE.
The sample was tilted relative to the neutron beam
direction by 45 deg.
20 um dE and 300 um E detector which
had 5 x 5 cm2 active area and 16 strips
Background due to 14N(n,p) in the sample backing was
subtracted with a dummy sample (i.e., backing without
the 7Be deposit), which is imporant above ~500 keV.
On+ED
Alberto Mengoni (2018-08-16):
The cross section was extracted by the following two
methods:
a) using the absolute flux as in M.Sabate-Gilarte
et al., Eur.Phys.J.A53(2017)210, and
b) using the ratio of the count rates (normalized to
the same neutron fluence) of the 7Be to 6Li
measured data and multiplying by the 6Li(n,t)
cross section data.
After verifying that both methods produced the same
result (within the uncertainty), we have operatively
adopted the data obtained with method (b), because it
reduced small fluctuations introduced by the neutron
flux.

- the (n,p) cross section derived from the (p,n) cross
section by Sekharan et al. (1976) with assumed
uncertainty of 5% above 35 keV.
By Alberto Mengoni (2019-02-12):
Most of the resonance parameters are purely fit
parameters, obtained to reproduce the best
representation of the adopted cross section.
They are not to be considered accurate physical
properties of the 8Be excited states.
